items.title: Hundreds pay last respects to offshore lifeboat victims
items.summary: The deceased, Nik Muhammad Hafifi Asri Ab Majid, 37, and Muhammad Faezuan Hakim Mohammad Bustamam, 28, were buried in separate graves. However, the rain eased as mourners, including family members, relatives and colleagues, proceeded into the burial ground when Nik Muhammad Hafifi's remains arrived at 5.30pm. Earlier, funeral prayers for Nik Muhammad Hafifi were held at the Imam Ash-Shafie Mosque in KotaSAS after his remains were brought from Sultanah Nur Zahirah Hospital in Terengganu, where post-mortem procedures were completed.

items.title: MARKET PULSE PM MAY 25, 2026 [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia's 30-stock index closed lower on Monday as investors adopt a cautious stance ahead of domestic banks' earnings performance this week. The key index dropped more than four points to 1,708. That wraps up today's Market Pulse.

items.title: Father hopes for breakthrough as hunt continues for missing teen at Bukit Changkat Asa
items.summary: Azizul Abdul Aziz, 48, said he and his family have come to terms with all possibilities as the disappearance of his daughter, Nur Izzati Humaira, 18, entered its third day today. He said Nur Izzati Humaira had recently started working at a restaurant near Maktab Rendah Sains MARA Sungkai and was also active in netball. “Even after finishing school, she would still return to her former secondary school voluntarily to help the sports teacher train the school team,” he said.

items.title: Penang records rise in korban permits ahead of Aidiladha
items.summary: State DVS director Dr Saira Banu Mohamed Rejab said the rise reflected greater community participation and increased organisation of korban activities across Penang. "Overall, 6,107 livestock have been recorded for korban activities this year compared with 4,220 animals in 2025, an increase of 1,887 livestock or 44.72 per cent," she said today. Dr Saira Banu said the livestock comprised 5,476 cattle, 26 buffaloes, 542 goats, 57 sheep and six camels.

items.title: Axiata sees better dividend from deleveraging, merger plans
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Axiata Group Bhd expects to see better dividend prospects from its operating companies as the group advances its deleveraging strategy and merger integration plans across key markets. Vivek said proceeds from any monetisation exercise would primarily be channelled towards reducing holding company debt, which could further strengthen its balance sheet and support future dividend growth. Meanwhile, Vivek said the group is also accelerating dividend repatriation from operating companies to reduce exposure to currency depreciation risks.

items.title: ITMAX Q1 net profit up 24.2pct to RM25mil on strong digital infrastructure growth
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: ITMAX System Bhd's net profit rose 24.2 per cent to RM25.01 million in the first quarter (Q1) ended March 31, 2026, from RM20.15 million a year ago. The digital infrastructure solutions segment remained the company's major revenue contributor, accounting for 83 per cent of the overall revenue. The segment recorded a 47.1 per cent increase in revenue to RM54.7 million.

items.title: Sepat oil platform tragedy: Autopsy shows abdominal injuries, drowning among causes of deaths
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU: Severe abdominal injuries from a powerful impact, neck injuries, and drowning have been identified as the causes of death for three victims of a lifeboat accident at the Sepat oil platform in Terengganu-Kelantan waters yesterday. The first victim, Nik Muhammad Hafifi Ab Majid, was confirmed to have died from severe abdominal injuries caused by a powerful impact. Meanwhile, Ahmad Fiqri Zakaria died by drowning.

items.title: 51 arrested in raids on drug-fuelled gay parties after man dies in KL
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A syndicate linked to drug abuse and immoral activities was dismantled after police conducted raids on upscale hotels here, arresting 51 individuals in the early hours yesterday. Bukit Aman Narcotics Crime Investigation Department (NCID) director Datuk Hussein Omar Khan said the operation was carried out jointly with the Kuala Lumpur and Brickfields district police headquarters NCID. "Police also seized various types of drugs, including 1.15kg of MDMA liquid, 284g of MDMA powder, ecstasy pills, ecstasy powder and ketamine," he said in a statement today.

items.title: Lagenda Properties records RM44.2mil Q1 net profit on stronger property sales
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Lagenda Properties Bhd posted revenue of RM262.1 million for the first quarter ended March 31, 2026 (Q1 FY26), while maintaining steady profitability with a net profit of RM44.2 million. Nevertheless, property development remained the company's main earnings driver, accounting for some 90 per cent of total revenue. "We are pleased to have delivered another quarter of resilient performance, supported by steady demand across our township developments.

items.title: Three injured in Bayan Baru two-car crash
items.summary: The Penang Fire and Rescue Department said one man was trapped while two others sustained minor injuries. "We received the call at 2.20pm and arrived five minutes later, finding one victim trapped in the driver's seat," said Penang Fire and Rescue Department assistant director (Operations) John Sagun Francis. He said the Proton Persona driver sustained serious injuries, while the 41-year-old Proton X50 driver and a female passenger suffered minor injuries.

items.title: Perak forms special committee as cable theft, vandalism threaten telecoms network and public safety
items.summary: IPOH, May 25 — The Perak State Government has established the Perak State Network Integrity and Security Committee to strengthen cooperation with agencies to address the issue of cable theft, open manholes and vandalism of telecommunications infrastructure. State Communications, Multimedia and NGO Committee chairman Mohd Azlan Helmi said the committee, which was established on May 12, will coordinate enforcement, monitoring and preventive actions related to the problem. “The committee involves the cooperation of the Perak State Legal Advisor’s Office, the State Economic Planning Unit (UPEN), the Local Government Division of the Perak State Secretary’s Office, the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC), police, Telekom Malaysia ™ and telecommunications service providers,” he said.

items.title: Excavator driver seriously injured after quarry landslide buries machine in Pokok Sena
items.summary: ALOR SETAR, May 25 — An excavator driver was seriously injured after it was buried by a quarry landslide at Simpang Bukit Tembaga near Pokok Sena today. Pokok Sena Fire and Rescue Station chief Senior Fire Officer II Bakar Saad said the department received an emergency call regarding the landslide involving soil and quarry rocks at 2.07 pm before a rescue team arrived at the location about seven minutes later. “Upon arrival, it was found that a landslide involving soil and quarry rocks had crushed an excavator, causing the driver to become trapped and pinned beneath the debris.

items.title: Cops probe viral video of bus driver handling liquid at wheel
items.summary: IPOH: Police are investigating a viral video showing an express bus driver pouring liquid into a bottle while driving, in an incident shared on social media platform Threads last Thursday. Taiping police chief Assistant Commissioner Abd Malek Ismail said its Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Division had received information on the video, which was recorded by a passenger before being uploaded online. Abd Malek urged witnesses, including the user who uploaded the video, to come forward to assist investigations.

items.title: Allianz Malaysia posts RM227.35mil net profit in Q1
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Allianz Malaysia Bhd posted a higher net profit of RM227.35 million in the first quarter of its financial year ended March 31, 2026 (Q1 2026), compared to RM213.04 million in Q1 2025. In a Bursa Malaysia filing, it said revenue rose to RM1.63 billion from RM1.53 billion in the same period last year, mainly due to higher insurance revenue from both insurance segments. "The life insurance segment recorded an insurance revenue of RM713.1 million for 1Q 2026, an increase of 7.0 per cent YoY, mainly due to higher revenue from investment-linked protection and employee benefit business," it said.

items.title: Topmix posts higher Q1 net profit of RM3.41mil, commits to 20pct dividend payout
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU: Decorative surface solutions provider Topmix Bhd (Topmix) kicked off the first quarter ended March 31, 2026, (Q1 2026) with an 11.5 per cent year-on-year (YoY) increase in profit after tax and minority interest (Patami) to RM3.41 million. Topmix, in a statement today, announced that its Q1 revenue grew 14.5 per cent YoY to RM25.3 million from RM22.1 million in the previous corresponding quarter, anchored by sustained demand for its high-pressure laminate products. Backed by higher volume contribution and a favourable product mix, Topmix's gross profit expanded 28.9 per cent YoY to RM11.5 million from RM8.9 million previously.

items.title: Suhakam findings point to breakdown in prison custodial practices, says criminologist
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Suhakam findings on the Taiping Prison transfer incident point not only to operational shortcomings but to a serious breakdown in the protection of human dignity and lawful custodial practices, says criminologist Datuk Dr P. Sundramoorthy. He said the findings indicated a failure to uphold legal and moral obligations owed to detainees under the Federal Constitution, prison regulations and international human rights standards. "Excessive force, if proven, represents an abuse of state authority and undermines public confidence in correctional institutions.

items.title: IHH deploys strategies to tackle rising costs
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: IHH Healthcare Bhd remains confident in its growth outlook despite rising medical cost pressures as the company implements efficiency-driven strategies and contingency measures to manage escalating medicine and operational costs. Speaking after its 16th Annual General Meeting, IHH group chief executive officer Dr Prem Kumar Nair said healthcare inflation remains an industry-wide challenge, with medical costs increasing by approximately two to three per cent across certain areas of its operations due to cost pressures beyond general inflation. He added that ambulatory care also helps hospitals optimise resources more efficiently amid rising healthcare demand and increasing case intensity across its markets.

items.title: Thunderstorms, heavy rain in nine states until 8pm
items.summary: It said in a statement that such weather would be experienced in Kelantan, Kedah (Baling, Kulim and Bandar Baharu), Perak (Larut, Matang and Selama, Hulu Perak and Kuala Kangsar), Pahang (Lipis, Raub, Jerantut, Bentong, Temerloh, Maran, Kuantan, Bera and Pekan) and Terengganu (Besut, Setiu, Hulu Terengganu, Marang, Dungun and Kemaman). Similar conditions are expected in Hulu Selangor in Selangor, as well as Jelebu and Jempol in Negri Sembilan. In Sarawak, the affected areas are Kuching (Lundu and Kuching), Samarahan, Sri Aman, Betong (Pusa and Betong), Sarikei (Pakan and Julau), Kapit (Song and Kapit), Miri (Beluru and Marudi) and Limbang.

items.title: Israel far-right minister calls for return to 'intensive' combat in Lebanon
items.summary: TEL AVIV: Israel's far-right national security minister, Itamar Ben Gvir, called Monday for a return to "intensive" fighting in Lebanon, despite a ceasefire aimed at halting hostilities between Israeli forces and Hizbollah. Israel responded with retaliatory attacks before launching a ground invasion into southern Lebanon. Israeli forces are currently operating inside an Israeli-announced "yellow line" running around 10 kilometres (six miles) deep along Lebanon's southern border.

items.title: Pope urges 'disarming' of artificial intelligence in major manifesto
items.summary: VATICAN CITY: Pope Leo XIV called Monday for the "disarming" of artificial intelligence in his long-awaited manifesto on the rapidly developing technology, and warned of "new forms of slavery" behind its rise. The "just war" theory – espoused recently by the Trump administration – was "outdated", Leo wrote in his first encyclical, which he presented in person at the Vatican, alongside AI experts including the co-founder of US giant Anthropic. "Disarming AI means freeing it from the mentality of 'armed' competition," the pope wrote in "Magnifica Humanitas" (Magnificent Humanity), a bid to address the ethical and social challenges behind AI.

items.title: Thunderstorms, strong winds and heavy rain forecast across nine states until 8pm
items.summary: It said in a statement that such weather would be experienced in Kelantan, Kedah (Baling, Kulim and Bandar Baharu), Perak (Larut, Matang and Selama, Hulu Perak and Kuala Kangsar), Pahang (Lipis, Raub, Jerantut, Bentong, Temerloh, Maran, Kuantan, Bera and Pekan) and Terengganu (Besut, Setiu, Hulu Terengganu, Marang, Dungun and Kemaman). Similar conditions are expected in Hulu Selangor in Selangor, as well as Jelebu and Jempol in Negeri Sembilan. In Sarawak, the affected areas are Kuching (Lundu and Kuching), Samarahan, Sri Aman, Betong (Pusa and Betong), Sarikei (Pakan and Julau), Kapit (Song and Kapit), Miri (Beluru and Marudi) and Limbang.

items.title: Father charged with murder of 2-month-old daughter in Labuan
items.summary: LABUAN: A 37-year-old man was charged in the magistrate's court here today with the murder of his two-month-old daughter at a house in Kampung Bukit Kuda earlier this month. No plea was recorded from the accused, Azmi Amat, after the charge was read before magistrate Dr Melissa Chia Pui Fung. A post-mortem examination found that the baby suffered severe head injuries consistent with blunt force trauma, as well as multiple rib fractures.

items.title: Pope calls idea of 'just war' put forward by US 'outdated'
items.summary: VATICAN CITY: Pope Leo XIV on Monday labelled "outdated" the concept of a "just war" put forward by US President Donald Trump's administration, warning that "humanity is slipping into a violent culture of power." The pope has been a high-profile critic of the Middle East war which started with US and Israeli air strikes on Iran in February. He condemned Trump's threat to destroy Iran as "truly unacceptable" and urged Americans to demand that US lawmakers "work for peace."

items.title: Shipping costs to Middle East jump up to 80pct, fertiliser prices surge 45pct
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's agri-commodity sector is facing a severe cost squeeze on multiple fronts, with shipping costs to the Middle East surging between 50 and 80 per cent and war risk insurance premiums rising to as much as three per cent, Economy Minister Datuk Seri Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said. Beyond logistics, upstream plantation and machinery costs have risen between 10 and 30 per cent, while rubber replanting costs have increased by between 46 and 55 per cent. Manufacturing costs are also under strain, with palm oleochemical production costs rising by up to 30 per cent.

items.title: As Stephen Colbert exits 'The Late Show,' podcasts have the last laugh
items.summary: STEPHEN Colbert's sign-off as the host of the CBS "The Late Show" may signal the end of a historic TV franchise, but the late-night tradition is far from dead. Comedy is booming on podcasts, where former late-night hosts like Conan O'Brien, Chelsea Handler and Samantha Bee have all found second acts. His podcast has attracted nearly 4.6 million subscribers on YouTube, more than 10 times higher than his Comedy Central show audience, which averaged a reported 372,000 viewers in his last year hosting the show.

items.title: High Court allows UOA arm to contest RM165 mil tax claim
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The High Court has granted UOA Development Bhd's investment arm, Distinctive Acres Sdn Bhd, leave to contest a RM165.66 million tax assessment and penalty arising from the 2020 sale of an asset in Bangsar South to a real estate investment trust (REIT). The dispute centres on IRB's notice of additional assessment dated Oct 10 last year over the disposal of UOA Corporate Tower in Bangsar South to UOA Real Estate Investment Trust in 2020. UOA Development, through Distinctive Acres, filed a judicial review application on Oct 22 last year, seeking leave from the court to quash the IRB's decision to issue the additional tax assessment notice.

items.title: Fadillah says Malaysia’s energy policies must evolve to meet rising green power demand
items.summary: He also said the government realised that the energy transition agenda was no longer just about the environment, but also involved aspects of economic competitiveness, high-quality investments, industrial development, energy security and national resilience. “The national energy policy is guided by the principle of the ‘energy trilemma’ which includes energy security, affordability and environmental sustainability. “Through this framework, consumers corporates can obtain renewable energy electricity directly from renewable energy developers without the need for dedicated private transmission infrastructure.

items.title: BAT Malaysia swings into the red in Q1
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: British American Tobacco (Malaysia) Bhd (BAT Malaysia) posted a loss in the first quarter ended March 31, 2026 dragged down by the retail display ban and rising illicit cigarette trade. This was accompanied by a 2.3 percentage points increase in illicit cigarette incidence, the first increase in illicit cigarette incidence since 2021, which continued to adversely affect BAT Malaysia's overall market share and industry dynamics. "The increase represents an estimated additional 22 million illicit cigarette sticks entering the market within a single quarter.

items.title: Security guard charged with murder of friend at Ijok landfill
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: A security guard was charged in the Kuala Selangor magistrate's court with the murder of his friend at a landfill in Ladang Tuan Mee, Ijok, early this month. K. Turairajoo, 43, nodded in understanding after the charge was read out before magistrate Mohamad Ibrahim Muhammad Ghulam. The man was charged with murdering S. Lachumanan, 23, at the Ladang Tuan Mee landfill in Ijok, within the Kuala Selangor district, between 4.41pm on May 11 and 4.42am on May 14.

items.title: Fallen trees disrupt traffic across KL after heavy rain and strong winds
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Heavy rain and strong winds caused fallen trees across the city this afternoon, disrupting traffic and damaging parked vehicles. The Kuala Lumpur Fire and Rescue Department Operations Command Centre said it received several emergency calls via the NG999 line at 2.50pm, prompting the deployment of rescuers to three locations. At Seri Malaysia Apartments along Jalan 3/141 in Kampung Malaysia Tambahan, a tree also fell onto a parked Proton Persona by the roadside, with no injuries reported.

items.title: Aeon Bank appoints Mohammad Ridzuan as CEO
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Aeon Bank (M) Bhd has appointed Mohammad Ridzuan Abdul Aziz as the new chief executive officer effective May 22, 2026. Prior to this, Ridzuan served as the independent non-executive director of Aeon Bank from February 2023 until February 2026. He was appointed as the bank's officer-in-charge effective April 1.

items.title: Queen visits historic Dolmabahce Palace in Istanbul
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Her Majesty Raja Zarith Sofiah, Queen of Malaysia, visited the Dolmabahce Palace Museum in Istanbul yesterday. According to a post on the Sultan Ibrahim Sultan Iskandar Facebook page, upon arrival at the museum, Her Majesty was received by the museum's Head of Protocol, Tangül Erol. Accompanying the Queen during the visit were the Tunku Temenggong of Johor, Tunku Idris Iskandar Al-Haj Ibni Sultan Ibrahim, the Tunku Panglima of Johor, Tunku Abdul Rahman Al-Haj Ibni Sultan Ibrahim and the Tunku Putera of Johor, Tunku Abu Bakar Al-Haj Ibni Sultan Ibrahim.

items.title: Holiday rush: Nearly 100,000 Langkawi ferry tickets sold
items.summary: ALOR STAR: A total of 99,128 ferry tickets for travel between Kuala Kedah, Kuala Perlis and Langkawi have been sold ahead of the upcoming Aidiladha celebrations and school holidays. Konsortium Langkawi Ferry Line Ventures Sdn Bhd operations manager Lt Cmdr (Rtd) Norhafiz Abdul Wahid said 120 regular trips and 53 additional trips had been scheduled from May 21 to June 7. Norhafiz said despite global geopolitical tensions and high fuel costs, demand for travel to Langkawi remained strong.

items.title: Johor elderly aid system sparks row between DAP and state govt [WATCH]
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: The distribution mechanism for Johor's Bantuan Kasih Johor (BKJ) welfare aid has sparked a political spat after DAP strategic director Liew Chin Tong called for reforms to make the system more efficient and elderly-friendly. The Iskandar Puteri MP and Perling assemblyman said recent implementation issues highlighted weaknesses that needed urgent attention, adding that welfare assistance should not burden recipients, particularly senior citizens required to attend physical collection exercises. "I read Liew Chin Tong's statement regarding BKJ for senior citizens.

items.title: One hiker airlifted from Gunung Batu Putih in Tapah, search continues for injured 49‑year‑old woman
items.summary: Perak Fire and Rescue Department director Datuk Sayani Saidon said the victim, Mohd Hanafi Neikmad, 41, was successfully brought down from the mountain peak and sent to Tapah Hospital at about 3pm. Without aircraft assistance, the operation would have been much more difficult as the journey to the peak of Gunung Batu Putih takes about 12 hours. “The victim was found and successfully rescued about one kilometre from the peak of Gunung Batu Putih, at an estimated altitude of around 2,000 metres and for the record, the peak of Gunung Batu Putih is 2,132 metres high,” he said in a statement.

items.title: UK set to break record for hottest May day
items.summary: LONDON: Britain was set to break its record for the hottest day in May on Monday, the national weather agency predicted, as the country sweltered in an "unprecedented" heatwave. "Today will be the hottest day in May in the UK in our temperature records, with highs of 35 degrees Celsius expected," the Met Office said on social media. The current record for the hottest May day is 32.8 degrees Celcius, set in 1922 and seen again in 1944.

items.title: MetMalaysia issues multiple thunderstorm alerts across Malaysia, warns of rain and strong winds until 8pm
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 25 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued multiple thunderstorm warnings today, forecasting heavy rain, thunderstorms and strong winds across several states until this evening. In its latest 5pm update posted on Facebook, MetMalaysia warned that thunderstorms are expected in Kedah, Perak, Kelantan, Terengganu, Pahang, Selangor, Negeri Sembilan, Sarawak and Sabah, with conditions expected to persist until 8pm on Monday, May 25. That earlier warning remains valid until 7pm today and covers Selangor, Negeri Sembilan, Terengganu, Pahang, Perak, Kelantan, Johor, Sabah and Sarawak, among others.

items.title: Judges say seven years fits the crime, restore jail term for motorcycle snatch theft case
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 25 — The Court of Appeal today reinstated a seven-year jail sentence imposed on a former factory worker who pleaded guilty to robbery, ruling that reckless offences endangering members of the public will face severe consequences. Justice Noorin said such offences are not isolated theft cases but crimes that undermine public confidence and safety in public spaces, particularly affecting women and vulnerable road users. “Courts have consistently recognised that such offences are not isolated acts of theft but offences which undermine public confidence in safety within public spaces,” she said.

items.title: No plea recorded as Labuan father charged with infant murder, court sets June date
items.summary: LABUAN, May 25 — A 37-year-old man was charged in the Magistrate’s Court here today with the murder of his two-month-old daughter at a house in Kampung Bukit Kuda early this month. No plea was recorded from the accused, Azmi Amat, after the charge was read before Magistrate Dr Melissa Chia Pui Fung. A post-mortem examination revealed that the baby had serious head injuries caused by blunt force trauma, as well as several rib fractures.

items.title: Financial issues behind delay in Air Itam-Tun Dr Lim Chong Eu Expressway project, says Penang CM
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 25 — Penang Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow has admitted that the delay in the completion of the Air Itam-Tun Dr Lim Chong Eu Expressway was due to financial issues. He said the project concessionaire was facing financial issues but they have been given an extension until next year to complete the project. “There are delays but works are still in progress, the final deadline is April next year,” he said when asked to comment on the delay in the project which physically started in 2021.

items.title: Viral Threads post of bus driver casually pouring drink while driving sparks police investigation in Taiping
items.summary: IPOH, May 25 — Police are investigating a viral video showing a driver pouring liquid into a bottle while driving a bus, which was shared by a social media user on Threads last Thursday. Taiping district police chief ACP Abd Malek Ismail said the Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Division of the Taiping district police headquarters had received information regarding the video, which was recorded by one of the bus passengers before being uploaded on social media. Abd Malek said police are also still tracing the bus company involved as well as the actual route taken by the bus, adding that the case could be investigated under Section 42 of the Road Transport Act 1987 for reckless and dangerous driving.

items.title: Still no decision on controversial RM1b Jelutong landfill project, says Penang CM
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 25 — The Penang government is yet to make any decision on the proposed RM1 billion Jelutong landfill rehabilitation and reclamation project pending presentation from Penang Development Corporation (PDC). Penang Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said PDC is yet to make a presentation on the project to the state exco. Previously, state exco Jason H’ng Mooi Lye said the developer, PLB Engineering Berhad, will be applying to project owner, Penang Development Corporation, for an extension of time (EOT).

items.title: Unexpected campus power outage delays Grand Asian Chess Challenge matches at Universiti Malaya
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 25 — The final day of the 27th Grand Asian Chess Challenge (GACC) Championship held at the Auditorium of the Kompleks Perdana Siswa (KPS), Universiti Malaya (UM), here, was disrupted following an unexpected power outage early this morning. The power outage, believed to have affected the entire UM campus, occurred at about 8.45 am during the final preparation phase before the first round commenced. Despite the disruption, he said no matches were postponed to another day and all remaining rounds were successfully completed today.

items.title: Tengku Zafrul to testify in Muhyiddin RM232.5m graft trial, money laundering case on July 6
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 25 — Former finance minister Datuk Seri Tengku Zafrul Tengku Abdul Aziz will be called as the 11th prosecution witness in the corruption and money laundering trial involving Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin. According to Astro Awani, deputy public prosecutor Datuk Wan Shaharuddin Wan Ladin informed the High Court that Tengku Zafrul will take the stand at the next proceedings fixed for July 6, after the 10th prosecution witness, R. Harshini, completed her testimony before judge Noor Ruwena Md Nurdin. The trial is set to continue on July 6–17, July 27–29, August 17–21, August 26–27, and September 1–3 and 14.

items.title: Haj pilgrims from Malaysia head to Arafah, 31,600 prepare for wukuf ritual tomorrow
items.summary: MAKKAH, May 25 — A total of 31,600 Malaysian Haj pilgrims, among the 1.5 million Muslims from around the world performing the pilgrimage this year, began moving to Arafah in stages from 7.30am today to prepare for the wukuf ritual tomorrow. Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Religious Affairs) Dr Zulkifli Hasan said the movement of pilgrims was proceeding smoothly and was expected to be completed by today. The pilgrims will begin their wukuf tomorrow,” he told reporters after witnessing the departure of pilgrims to Arafah today.

items.title: Moody’s notes Johor integrates into Singapore strategies, Malaysia emerges as fast‑growing data centre market
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 25 — Malaysia, particularly Johor, has positioned itself as a cost-efficient alternative to Singapore for data centre investments, owing to its relatively abundant land, availability of power infrastructure, and attractive investment incentives. Additionally, Moody’s Ratings said Malaysia, one of the fastest-growing data centre markets in South and Southeast Asia, has benefited from available power and transmission infrastructure, supported by its solid power reserve margin, enabling rapid scaling of data centre capacity. “Failure to deliver on these power sector initiatives will likely constrain Malaysia’s ability to support further growth in data centre development, as power demand from data centres is already significant and increasing.

items.title: SAR team expands search radius to 0.5 square kilometres for missing Hulu Selangor teen hiker
items.summary: HULU SELANGOR, May 25 — The search and rescue (SAR) operation for a 19-year-old girl believed to have gone missing during a hiking activity at Bukit Changkat Asa here entered its third day today, with the search radius expanded to 0.5 square kilometres. “Today’s operation also involves the use of drone units from the Selangor Government Disaster Management Unit,” she said. Azliza added that agencies involved in the operation included the Malaysia Defence Force (APM), the Forestry Department, the Department of Wildlife and National Parks Peninsular Malaysia (Perhilitan), and the Forestry Mountain Guide Association.

items.title: Second teenager’s body found floating near Dungun jetty after Sungai Beril drowning tragedy
items.summary: DUNGUN, May 25 — The body of the second teenager who drowned in Sungai Beril near Taman Beril, Paka, on Sunday was found this morning. Dungun acting district police chief DSP Jumaidy Bah Chong Weh said members of the public spotted the body of Muhammad Aidil Amsyar Mohd Zul-Zunaidi, 14, floating near the Paka Market jetty at 9.55am. He said the body of the Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan Agama Durian Guling student was located about two kilometres from where the incident occurred.

items.title: Anwar pledges targeted Budi Madani support for smallholders, entrepreneurs, vulnerable groups
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 25 — The government has agreed to continue the Budi Madani: Budi Agri-Komoditi initiative in a targeted manner to assist smallholders, entrepreneurs and vulnerable groups who are the backbone of the country’s economy. “Based on these considerations, the government has agreed to continue the Budi Madani: Budi Agri-Komoditi initiative in a targeted manner to assist smallholders, entrepreneurs and vulnerable groups who are the backbone of the country’s economy,” he said in a Facebook post today. Regarding the people’s health security, Anwar said that overall, the supply of medicines in the country is still stable and MOH will continue to closely monitor any challenges involving the stock chain and prices to prevent the people from becoming victims of the situation.

items.title: DOSM says 587,801 job vacancies advertised online in Q4 2025, services sector leads
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 25 — A total of 587,801 job vacancies were advertised online in the fourth quarter of 2025, according to the Department of Statistics Malaysia (DOSM) in its publication Big Data Analytics: Job Market Insights – Online Job Vacancies in Malaysia, Q4 2025 . Chief Statistician Datuk Seri Dr Mohd Uzir Mahidin said the number of companies advertising jobs rose to 80,287 in the fourth quarter from 77,615 in the previous quarter. Mohd Uzir added that the services sector remained the largest contributor, accounting for 84.4 per cent or 496,065 online job advertisements in the fourth quarter, compared with 77.2 per cent in the previous quarter.

items.title: Pasir Mas ustaz faces four charges of sexually assaulting boys, producing obscene images
items.summary: PASIR MAS, May 25 — An ustaz, or a religious teacher, pleaded not guilty in the Magistrate’s Court here today to four charges of sexually assaulting two boys and producing obscene images. Muhammad Faiz Muhamad Ramly, 31, entered the plea before Magistrate Syed Farid Syed Ali. For the third and fourth charges, he was accused of committing physical sexual assault on a six-year-old boy and producing obscene images involving the child at a house in Kampung Layat, Jeli, in May 2025.

items.title: Malaysia deepens defence ties with Türkiye as LMS Batch 2 programme launched in Istanbul
items.summary: ISTANBUL, May 25 — Malaysia has strengthened its defence-industrial cooperation with Türkiye through a series of engagements during the defence minister’s official working visit, which culminated in the naming and launching of the Royal Malaysian Navy’s Littoral Mission Ship (LMS) Batch 2. Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said the visit reflects growing trust and strategic cooperation between both countries, particularly in defence technology and industrial development. Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin during a meeting with Desan in Turkiye.

items.title: Hannah Yeoh: Food courts, hawker stalls and markets in Putrajaya to see rental rates reduced
items.summary: Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh said the initiative, expected to benefit 244 petty traders in Putrajaya, was introduced to ease operational burdens amid the still challenging economic climate. Hannah said traders wishing to qualify for the rental reduction must first settle any outstanding rental arrears up to May 2026. “As the people continue facing various daily challenges, targeted assistance is essential to ensure petty traders can continue to survive,” she said.

items.title: ‘Purchasing power is in your hands’: Economy minister urges Malaysians to compare prices amid rising cost concerns
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 25 — Putrajaya today urged Malaysians to compare prices and make informed purchasing decisions amid the ongoing global supply pressures linked to the West Asia conflict. Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said the government is continuing weekly monitoring of domestic food prices while encouraging the public to use the government-run PriceCatcher mobile application to check and compare prices before making purchases. Compare prices.

items.title: Eleven men charged with alleged ‘RT6 Gang’ membership in KL Sessions Court
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 25 — A total of 11 men were charged in the Sessions Court here today with being members of an organised crime group known as the “RT6 Gang” since 2020. They are A. Raja Ratanam, K. Sunthreson, C. Vijaya Kumar, N. Nadarajah, T. Raman, R. Vijayan, M. Kumaaran, N. Harichandran, S. Maran, Suwaran Singh Minder Singh and Mohammad Atif Khan Mohammad Ali Khan, aged between 22 and 48. The prosecution was conducted by Deputy Public Prosecutors Lokman Kasim and Yazid Mustaqim Roslan, while the defence team comprised lawyers Datuk Rajpal Singh, Eu Kah Mun, M. Mathaven, Grace Francis, Muhammad Izzhar Idris, S. Kirthen, S. Premila and Kelvin Francis.

items.title: Sepat FSO tragedy: Police conduct detailed investigation into deaths of three workers
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU, May 25 — Police are conducting a detailed investigation into the accident that claimed the lives of three workers at the Sepat floating storage and offloading (FSO) facility, located about 150 metres off the waters of Kuala Terengganu, yesterday afternoon. Kuala Terengganu police chief ACP Azli Mohd Noor said a team of officers from the Criminal Investigation Department had been dispatched to the location to determine the cause of the incident. — Bernama

items.title: Malaysia’s oil supply remains stable until July, Petronas assures amid global energy tensions, says minister
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 25 — Petronas has assured Putrajaya that Malaysia’s national oil supply stock is sufficient until July 2026 despite ongoing instability in West Asia and continued tensions around the Strait of Hormuz, Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said today. Akmal said the average Brent crude oil price between May 18 and 22 stood at US$111.67 (RM441.74) per barrel, up 1.7 per cent from US$109.85 the previous week. Coal prices also edged up marginally by 0.7 per cent to US$132.29 per metric tonne.

items.title: Man accused of stabbing woman in the neck in Kedah car attack to be charged tomorrow
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 25 — A man suspected of stabbing his female friend in the neck inside a car in Titi Gajah, Kepala Batas in Alor Setar, Kedash, on May 17 will be charged tomorrow. Kota Setar district police chief Assistant Commissioner Syed Basri Syed Ali confirmed the matter, saying the suspect will be charged at the Alor Setar Sessions Court, Harian Metro reported today. The suspect fled the scene but was arrested by police four hours later.

items.title: Kedah mechanic charged with three firearms offences in Sik Sessions Court, pleads not guilty
items.summary: SIK, May 25 — A mechanic was once again charged at the Sessions Court here today on three counts involving the possession of firearms, and pleaded not guilty to all charges. Mohd Shahifuldin Mohd Shahiran, 46, entered the plea before Judge Rohaida Ishak. On the third count, Mohd Shahifuldin was charged with possessing an imitation Glock 19 Gen 4 pistol and one bullet cartridge without lawful reason at the same place, time and date.

items.title: Malaysia’s medicine supply stable and sufficient despite Iran conflict, says economy minister
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 25 — Malaysia’s supply of medicines and medical devices remains stable and sufficient despite ongoing global supply disruptions linked to the Iran conflict, with only 16.8 per cent of medicine items currently classified as high risk, Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said today. “From the overall recorded medicine items, 72 per cent are at low risk, 11.3 per cent at medium risk, and 16.8 per cent at high risk requiring continuous monitoring and mitigation measures,” he said. For medical devices, he said 81.7 per cent were categorised as low risk, 13.5 per cent as medium risk, while only 4.8 per cent were considered high risk.
